title: Assignments for Improved Performance
breadcrumbs: Deployment Considerations for Performance and Scalability
text: Assignments for Improved Performance If many client processes access one server machine, both client and server might run out of memory. To avoid this problem and reduce the number of server processes started, make changes in the client assignment file: Remove the $SEARCH_DESCRIPTOR and $SEARCH_OBJECT settings. Make services ( *.svc , udesc.urr , and uobj.dol available on the client instead of on the server. To get a better overview of assignments for each server, change the assignment files as follows: For the server assignment files, create separate assignment files for database, application server, and environment. For example: database.asn , sasv.asn and env.asn In the client assignment file, update the UST for database actions to DBMS and for file access to SASV. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
